creator: scheidell@FreeBSD.org
text:
While investigating a problem with mail/smtp-cli in pr ports/168381, I removed the ipv6 perl module, just to make sure it 
wasn't causing some of the problems. (it wasn't).

smtp-cli includes code to work with systems that are compiled WITH_IPV6, and this code is included even in systems 
WITHOUT_IPV6.

This causes (sometimes) strange socket connections, depending on gateways, or other dependencies, (and includes a module you 
don't need)

Fix: - Honor WITHOUT_IPV6 option knob
- No PORTREVISION bump

upward compatible, won't do anything on default systems (WITH_IPV6), but does reduce modules needed.

I won't be offended if you don't want to approve this.



______________________________________________________________________
This email has been scanned and certified safe by SpammerTrap(r). 
For Information please see http://www.spammertrap.com/
______________________________________________________________________--kmoLJqx5oXhLuIEqSV1IzD5UHVHd3P9DpN6NRG1YO5RndjcS
Content-Type: text/plain; name="smtp-cli.patch"
Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit
Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="smtp-cli.patch"

Index: Makefile
===================================================================
RCS file: /home/pcvs/ports/mail/smtp-cli/Makefile,v
retrieving revision 1.4
diff -u -r1.4 Makefile
--- Makefile	8 Mar 2012 10:28:09 -0000	1.4
+++ Makefile	11 Jun 2012 19:58:47 -0000
@@ -18,19 +18,24 @@
 
 RUN_DEPENDS=	p5-Digest-HMAC>0:${PORTSDIR}/security/p5-Digest-HMAC \
 		p5-File-Type>0:${PORTSDIR}/devel/p5-File-Type \
-		p5-IO-Socket-INET6>0:${PORTSDIR}/net/p5-IO-Socket-INET6 \
 		p5-IO-Socket-SSL>0:${PORTSDIR}/security/p5-IO-Socket-SSL \
 		p5-MIME-Lite>0:${PORTSDIR}/mail/p5-MIME-Lite \
 		p5-Term-ReadKey>0:${PORTSDIR}/devel/p5-Term-ReadKey
 
+.if !defined(WITHOUT_IPV6)
+RUN_DEPENDS+=	p5-IO-Socket-INET6>0:${PORTSDIR}/net/p5-IO-Socket-INET6
+.else
+EXTRA_PATCHES+=${PATCHDIR}/extra-patch-NOIPV6
+.endif
+
 NO_BUILD=	yes
 WRKSRC=		${WRKDIR}
 
 PLIST_FILES=	bin/smtp-cli
 
 do-extract:
-	${MKDIR} ${WRKSRC}/
-	${CP} ${DISTDIR}/${DISTFILES} ${WRKSRC}/${DISTNAME}
+	@${MKDIR} ${WRKSRC}/
+	@${CP} ${DISTDIR}/${DISTFILES} ${WRKSRC}/${DISTNAME}
 
 post-patch:
 	@${REINPLACE_CMD} -e '1 s|#!/usr/bin/perl|#!${PERL}|' ${WRKSRC}/${DISTNAME}
Index: files/extra-patch-NOIPV6
===================================================================
RCS file: files/extra-patch-NOIPV6
diff -N files/extra-patch-NOIPV6
--- /dev/null	1 Jan 1970 00:00:00 -0000
+++ files/extra-patch-NOIPV6	11 Jun 2012 19:58:47 -0000
@@ -0,0 +1,58 @@
+--- smtp-cli.orig	2012-06-11 08:46:54.000000000 -0400
++++ smtp-cli	2012-06-11 08:49:01.000000000 -0400
+@@ -109,7 +109,6 @@
+ 	'host|server=s' => \$host,
+ 	'port=i' => \$port, 
+ 	'4|ipv4' => sub { $addr_family = AF_INET; },
+-	'6|ipv6' => sub { $addr_family = AF_INET6; },
+ 	'user=s' => \$user, 'password=s' => \$pass,
+ 	'auth-login' => \$use_login, 
+ 	'auth-plain' => \$use_plain,
+@@ -153,20 +152,6 @@
+ 	$missing_modules_count += 2;
+ }
+ 
+-## IO::Socket::INET6 and Socket6 are optional
+-my $socket6 = eval { require IO::Socket::INET6; require Socket6; 1; };
+-if (not $socket6) {
+-	if ($addr_family == AF_INET6) {
+-		die("!!! IO::Socket::INET6 and Socket6 modules are not found\nIPv6 support is not available\n");
+-	}
+-	if (not $missing_modules_ok) {
+-		warn("!!! IO::Socket::INET6 -- optional module not found\n");
+-		warn("!!! Socket6 -- optional module not found\n");
+-		warn("!!! These modules are required for IPv6 support\n\n");
+-		$missing_modules_count += 2;
+-	}
+-}
+-
+ ## MIME::Lite dependency is optional
+ my $mime_lite = eval { require MIME::Lite; 1; };
+ if (not $mime_lite and not $missing_modules_ok) {
+@@ -338,16 +323,10 @@
+ 	PeerPort => $port,
+ 	Proto => 'tcp',
+ 	Timeout => 5);
+-if ($socket6) {
+-	$connect_args{'Domain'} = $addr_family;
+-	$sock = IO::Socket::INET6->new(%connect_args) or die ("Connect failed: $@\n");
+-} else {
+ 	$sock = IO::Socket::INET->new(%connect_args) or die ("Connect failed: $@\n");
+-}
+ 
+ if ($verbose >= 1) {
+ 	my $addr_fmt = "%s";
+-	$addr_fmt = "[%s]" if ($sock->sockhost() =~ /:/); ## IPv6 connection
+ 
+ 	printf ("Connection from $addr_fmt:%s to $addr_fmt:%s\n",
+ 		$sock->sockhost(), $sock->sockport(),
+@@ -860,9 +839,6 @@
+                                 (default: 25)
+ 
+         -4 or --ipv4            Use standard IP (IPv4) protocol.
+-        -6 or --ipv6            Use IPv6 protocol. For hosts that have
+-                                both IPv6 and IPv4 addresses the IPv6
+-                                connection is tried first.
+         
+         --hello-host=<string>   String to use in the EHLO/HELO command.
+         --disable-ehlo          Don't use ESMTP EHLO command, only HELO.
How-To-Repeat: 
install smtp-cli on a system WITHOUT_IPV6, you will notice useless IPV6 module installed
